jokes.text: Oh god, I love to smoke. we're live at the opening of the latest chapter of the epic space saga, cosmic wars! And the nerds have emerged from their basements, wearing strange costumes to shield their pasty skin from the moonlight.
jokes.setup: News reporter covering movie premiere
jokes.punchline: Describes nerds as vampire-like creatures shielding from moonlight
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about fan culture with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Your ignorance is amusing, and sad. I am dressed in the actual clothing worn by cosmic wars creator randall curtis. I bought them at auction, then added pregnancy panels to fit my unique body type.
jokes.setup: Being accused of not having a costume
jokes.punchline: Wearing creator's actual clothes modified with pregnancy panels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy character moment - pompous, obsessive, and hilariously specific about his 'unique body type'

jokes.text: One ticket for the space show. I want to see if any of them aliens match up to the one I got in my root cellar.
jokes.setup: Buying movie ticket
jokes.punchline: Wants to compare movie aliens to his actual alien in root cellar
jokes.explanation: Classic Cletus - casually mentions having an alien like it's normal farm equipment

jokes.text: Aw, man! I waited three weeks at the wrong ticket window!
jokes.setup: Someone trying to buy tickets to wrong movie
jokes.punchline: Waited three weeks at wrong window
jokes.explanation: Simple situational comedy, competent but not memorable

jokes.text: Man, even i think movie popcorn has gotten too big. Ow! Get your own, mooch!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on popcorn size
jokes.punchline: Someone trying to take his oversized popcorn
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about movie theater culture with Homer's typical hypocrisy

jokes.text: amendments?! Regulatory agencies?! What the fark-bot? Don't worry, they're just getting the plot out of the way so it won't slow down the... (imitates laser fire) (imitates light saber) (imitates wookie moaning)
jokes.setup: Movie showing boring political dialogue
jokes.punchline: Bart making sound effects for expected action scenes
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on Star Wars prequels with Bart's childlike impatience

jokes.text: Whoa, mama! Finally, some action!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about action in the movie
jokes.punchline: It's just more boring senate procedures
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Star Wars prequel criticism

jokes.text: Oh, I'm so bored! Maybe I'll clean out my wallet. Hey, my car insurance expired. Quite a while ago. (Laughing)
jokes.setup: Homer bored during movie, cleaning wallet
jokes.punchline: Discovers expired insurance and laughs about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - irresponsible about important things but finds it funny

jokes.text: I didn't realize british coal miners had it so bad. There's blood on your hands, mrs. Thatcher!
jokes.setup: Lisa watching what appears to be historical drama
jokes.punchline: Makes political commentary about Thatcher
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lisa's political awareness and moral outrage

jokes.text: jim-jam, what happened to the wheel covers on my landing gear? Me-sa sell them to buy me-sa some space spliff.
jokes.setup: Space movie dialogue about missing parts
jokes.punchline: Character sold them for space drugs
jokes.explanation: Parodies Jar Jar Binks with drug reference - solid but predictable

jokes.text: That character is just a tired stereotype. Yes, and it's-a makin' a-me so mad, I'm-a gonna throw the meatballs at-a screen. But first I gotta pose for a pizza box.
jokes.setup: Complaint about stereotypical character
jokes.punchline: Italian man responds with even more extreme stereotype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about stereotypes while deploying an even more ridiculous stereotype

jokes.text: That sucked! I can't believe the 'gathering shadow' was senate redistricting! Worst cosmic wars ever. I will only see it three more times. Today.
jokes.setup: Complaining movie was terrible
jokes.punchline: Will see it three more times today despite hating it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - captures obsessive fan behavior perfectly with escalating commitment despite criticism

jokes.text: From now on, I'm not looking forward to anything. Oh, my god! Tomorrow, there's a two-for-one sale on piano benches! I can't wait! Ooh, ooh, ooh!
jokes.setup: Homer declaring he won't look forward to anything
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets excited about mundane sale
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer contradiction - makes grand declaration then immediately breaks it over something trivial

jokes.text: kids, why don't you write a complaint letter? That's how I got the channel six weather girl to start wearing a bra. That was you!?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting writing complaint letter
jokes.punchline: Homer's previous complaint about weather girl's underwear
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate complaint history revealed, with good cringe factor

jokes.text: your movie stunk smelly butt. I am fine. Sincerely... I'll write the letter.
jokes.setup: Bart dictating complaint letter
jokes.punchline: Lisa takes over after Bart's childish attempt
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing Bart's immaturity vs Lisa's competence

jokes.text: Marge, you destroyed my interest in weather!
jokes.setup: Reference to previous weather girl complaint
jokes.punchline: Blames Marge for his lost interest in weather
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that shows Homer's tendency to blame others for his problems

jokes.text: 742 evergreen terrace. Dad, that's our address. (Whimpering): He's in the house?
jokes.setup: Looking up creator's address
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks creator is in their house
jokes.explanation: Homer's logical confusion about addresses creates brief comedic moment

jokes.text: Well, northern california is wine country. We could take a tour. And it's a perfect time. I just got fired again.
jokes.setup: Planning trip to Northern California
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions getting fired again
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual attitude toward job loss perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: I mean, who am I gonna complain about while I'm drinking? We should do more together. You're my best friend, homie. Well, yeah, legally.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about drinking with Marge
jokes.punchline: Agrees they're best friends 'legally'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about marriage with Homer's typical emotional detachment

jokes.text: But if we're really best friends, how come we never play hoops? The doctor said if you tried running again, your ankles would shatter. But you have to catch me first! (Bone cracks) oh! Oh, my ankle snapped!
jokes.setup: Homer challenging Marge to chase him despite ankle warning
jokes.punchline: Immediately injures ankle as predicted
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy setup and immediate payoff showcasing Homer's impulsiveness

jokes.text: It's a hip-hop loaf of garlic bread for an olive garden commercial. Yo, yo, yo! Entrees start at $6.95. Offer not good on sunday, fool.
jokes.setup: Expecting to see fantastic creatures being created
jokes.punchline: It's just rapping garlic bread for commercial
jokes.explanation: Absurd commercial parody that subverts expectations about movie magic

jokes.text: Unless it has too many fingers, which are tough.
jokes.setup: Explaining they can create any creature
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary limitation about finger difficulty
jokes.explanation: Absurd practical limitation in fantasy context

jokes.text: Ah, summer in tuscany.
jokes.setup: Describing wine country climate with exhaust fumes
jokes.punchline: Romantically compares it to Tuscany
jokes.explanation: Ironic contrast between romantic description and polluted reality

jokes.text: mmm, it goes great with the jolly rancher I'm sucking on!
jokes.setup: Wine tasting experience
jokes.punchline: Homer mixing wine with candy
jokes.explanation: Homer's lack of sophistication disrupting wine culture moment

jokes.text: Pleasant aroma, rich full body, well-aged... and the wine's not bad either. (Both guffawing) oh, stop ieinfeld.
jokes.setup: Homer describing wine characteristics
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was describing Marge, not the wine
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke, competent execution but predictable

jokes.text: we always get the hicks on saturdays.
jokes.setup: Wine tour guide observing Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: Dismisses them as typical Saturday hicks
jokes.explanation: Simple class-based humor, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Better technology doesn't mean better storytelling. Well, now i know you're crazy. Wait, before you have us killed, hear us out. I will wait ten of your earth seconds.
jokes.setup: Lisa making valid criticism about movies
jokes.punchline: Curtis threatens to kill them, offers ten seconds
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal conversation to absurd threat with specific time limit

jokes.text: I'm going back to my roots: Plots and characters lifted from westerns and samurai films. To the video store!
jokes.setup: Curtis promising to return to better storytelling
jokes.punchline: Admits his 'original' ideas were stolen from other genres
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Lucas/Star Wars, openly admitting 'original' work was derivative

jokes.text: Kids, please accept these boxes of jim-jam cereal. It's just alpha-bits with extra j'S.
jokes.setup: Curtis offering promotional cereal
jokes.punchline: Lazy cereal is just alphabet cereal with more J's
jokes.explanation: Absurd product tie-in that's both lazy and oddly specific

jokes.text: Well, we've learned if you don't like something, just go to the office and complain. What's our next stop? Fox broadcasting, 10201 west pico, building 203.
jokes.setup: Lesson about complaining to fix things
jokes.punchline: Next target is Fox Broadcasting (the show's own network)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where The Simpsons threatens to complain to their own network, with specific real address

jokes.text: I drank this much! (Bottles rattling) mom's not gonna like this. Don't tell mama what mama don't like.
jokes.setup: Homer showing how much he drank
jokes.punchline: Marge using broken grammar to enable him
jokes.explanation: Shows early signs of codependent drinking relationship

jokes.text: wow... mom and dad sure are having fun. Hey, as long as they're not hurting anybody. Uh-oh. Whoa! Aah! Please stop! Aah! I'll give you money! Aah! Okay, I'm going to tell! Ow! Aah!
jokes.setup: Kids thinking parents are having harmless fun
jokes.punchline: Cut to people being terrorized by drunk parents
jokes.explanation: Great visual juxtaposition between kids' innocent view and dark reality

jokes.text: Homie, you are so cute. (Giggling) oh, honey, I love to hear you laugh. (Imitating marge's giggle) I don't talk like that. (Imitating marge): Yes, you do.
jokes.setup: Drunk romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible impression of Marge's laugh
jokes.explanation: Sweet drunk moment with Homer's characteristic bad impressions

jokes.text: Well, you talk like... (imitating homer): Oh, marge, sorry I set the bed on fire. D-ohh! (Both laughing)
jokes.setup: Marge doing impression of Homer in return
jokes.punchline: References Homer setting bed on fire
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their playful dynamic while referencing Homer's history of disasters

jokes.text: That's your fun place, like me and the lamp store. Come on, we'll have a blast. Kids, while we're out, the tv's in charge. Go to bed when it says.
jokes.setup: Marge comparing Moe's to her own interests
jokes.punchline: Her fun place is a lamp store, TV babysits kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character beat - domestic interests and casual neglect of parenting duties

jokes.text: Wine? Geez. No one ever orders that. Umm... all I got is this old stuff here. 'Chateau latour-- 1886?' Oh, I should just throw this out. No, it'll have to do. That'll be four bucks.
jokes.setup: Moe dismissing wine request
jokes.punchline: Casually serves extremely expensive vintage wine for four dollars
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - his ignorance leads to serving priceless wine as house swill

jokes.text: Oh, what have I done? Let me dry my tears with this lost shakespeare play. (Sobbing)
jokes.setup: Moe realizing he gave away expensive wine
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having lost Shakespeare play as tissue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Moe's bar apparently contains priceless historical artifacts used carelessly

jokes.text: Like scott and zelda munster.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reference famous party couples
jokes.punchline: Confuses Scott/Zelda Fitzgerald with Munster family
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism mixing literary figures with TV monsters

jokes.text: Why are your keys in my scrambled eggs? And why is krusty on the couch? This'll cover what I did to the fireplace. (Groans)
jokes.setup: Morning after Homer and Marge's drinking
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd evidence of night's destruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalating evidence of drunk chaos with random celebrity appearance

jokes.text: we're all alone, no chaperone can't get our number the world's in slumber let's misbehave
jokes.setup: Drunk singing performance
jokes.punchline: Off-key drunk duet of old song
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk singing scene, serves story but not particularly funny

jokes.text: do you have to make such an unholy racket? Sorry, marge-- I got to break in my ski boots some time.
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about noise during hangover
jokes.punchline: Homer wearing ski boots in the house
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical behavior during sensitive moment shows his obliviousness

jokes.text: And when I feel weak, I will draw strength from the bible. Uh-oh. Here comes the gospel according to puke.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to get strength from Bible
jokes.punchline: About to vomit while holding it
jokes.explanation: Good blasphemous humor with Homer's physical comedy timing

jokes.text: duffmensch orders you to party! This reich will last a thousand beers. Oh, ja! I do this, and I'm jewish.
jokes.setup: German beer mascot with Nazi references
jokes.punchline: Jewish person participating despite obvious discomfort
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cultural insensitivity and personal compromise for fun

jokes.text: Ah, the germans. You just can't stay mad at 'em.
jokes.setup: Observing Nazi-themed beer celebration
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual forgiveness of historical atrocities
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Homer moment - his ignorance leads to casually dismissing genocide for beer

jokes.text: Well, I don't want to be a 'gloom-hilda.' I guess one beer won't hurt. Isn't that a little big? You can just nurse it. (Chanting): Nurse it! Nurse it!
jokes.setup: Marge reluctantly agreeing to one beer
jokes.punchline: Gets enormous beer with crowd encouraging slow consumption
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Marge's drinking escalation with crowd enabling

jokes.text: Have you ever walked on stilts? It's not that great. Yes, and you've said that several times now.
jokes.setup: Homer making conversation while drunk
jokes.punchline: Marge pointing out his repetitive drunk rambling
jokes.explanation: Realistic drunk behavior with Marge's increasing annoyance

jokes.text: I'm in no condition to drive. Wait-- I shouldn't listen to myself. I'm drunk. (Engine starts)
jokes.setup: Homer recognizing he shouldn't drive drunk
jokes.punchline: Decides not to trust his own judgment because he's drunk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic joke about drunk decision-making leading to terrible choice

jokes.text: Remember the rules for drinking and driving. Drive slow but not too slow. Drink some cola to keep yourself alert.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to drive drunk responsibly
jokes.punchline: His 'rules' include drinking more while driving
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drunk driving with Homer's twisted logic making it worse

jokes.text: Nice work, cruise control.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently successfully driving drunk
jokes.punchline: Credits cruise control for doing the driving
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete abdication of responsibility to car technology

jokes.text: If I get one more D.U.I., They'll take away my license. And what will I leave when I rent erblades?
jokes.setup: Homer worried about losing license
jokes.punchline: Main concern is what to use as deposit for Rollerblades
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities are completely backwards - worried about recreational deposits over serious consequences

jokes.text: oh, boy, I smell beer. Devil's mouthwash. You weren't calling it that at the christmas party.
jokes.setup: Wiggum dramatically condemning beer
jokes.punchline: Someone calls out his hypocrisy from Christmas party
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment with someone holding Wiggum accountable

jokes.text: Oh, my god! This D.U.I. Is a she-U.I.!
jokes.setup: Wiggum discovering DUI suspect is female
jokes.punchline: Makes up 'she-UI' terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum wordplay showing his strange relationship with law enforcement terminology

jokes.text: I'm going to be incarcer.. incarcer... incarer... I'm going to jail. (Wood snapping) what was that? Maybe someone else is here. And maybe he'll step forward and admit to being the real culprit. (Homer makes bird noises) bawk... ooh-ooh.
jokes.setup: Marge struggling with word 'incarcerated'
jokes.punchline: Homer making bird noises hoping to create distraction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from Marge's vocabulary struggle to Homer's desperate distraction attempts

jokes.text: Oh, this is a new low for me.
jokes.setup: Homer making bird noises to avoid responsibility
jokes.punchline: Even he recognizes this as pathetic
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of self-awareness about his behavior

jokes.text: Well, it's actually just a stepladder by the water fountain.
jokes.setup: Moe mentioning installing bidet for Marge
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a stepladder by water fountain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - his cheap, inappropriate solution presented as legitimate amenity

jokes.text: oh! What are you? You're like a monster! That's, like, the worst thing I've ever heard anybody do to anybody.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming to have heard everything as bartender
jokes.punchline: Immediately horrified by Homer's confession
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of bartender wisdom trope - even Moe is shocked

jokes.text: You should be drinking watered-down beer in a chipped glass on a stool with a nail sticking up out of it. Yeah, all right. But I get to poke you with a stick. (Both grunting)
jokes.setup: Moe prescribing punishment for Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer accepts but Moe adds stick-poking privilege
jokes.explanation: Moe's creative punishment escalates to casual sadism

jokes.text: Hey, save some for the rest of us, alky! Don't bogart our lord!
jokes.setup: Marge taking communion wine
jokes.punchline: Congregation treating communion like drug sharing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant blasphemous humor mixing religious ritual with drug culture terminology

jokes.text: Today's service is concluded. Suggest you all get on the road before marge.
jokes.setup: Normal church service ending
jokes.punchline: Warns congregation about Marge's drunk driving
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from religious authority figure about drunk driving safety

jokes.text: This is a pamphlet for chimney sweeping. Is it, marge? Is it? Oh, it is.
jokes.setup: Barney trying to give helpful pamphlet
jokes.punchline: Gets confused about what his own pamphlet is for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - his alcoholism affects even his attempts to help others

jokes.text: At oktoberfest, all I could think about was beer! I couldn't even celebrate... the harvest.
jokes.setup: Marge confessing drinking problem at Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: Thinks Oktoberfest is about harvest, not beer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety - doesn't understand beer festival is actually about beer

jokes.text: Sir, she is not an alcoholic! You can't put me on hold! I'll put you on hold! I am a lineman for the county
jokes.setup: Homer arguing with rehab facility
jokes.punchline: Gets into hold war while Glen Campbell song plays
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of phone system frustration with musical element

jokes.text: Mom never drives drunk, and the crash was in your car. Also, the driver's seat was adjusted for your stomach...
jokes.setup: Lisa presenting logical evidence
jokes.punchline: Driver's seat adjusted for Homer's specific body type
jokes.explanation: Lisa's detective work with specific physical evidence about Homer's gut

jokes.text: your suspicions are important to me and will be answered in the order received. Macarthur park is melting in the dark
jokes.setup: Homer deflecting Lisa's questions
jokes.punchline: Treats her like customer service while singing
jokes.explanation: Homer's absurd avoidance strategy mixing corporate speak with song

jokes.text: Hey, baby, you want to play caribbean stud? I'll show you what I'm holding. Get away from me! Your loss, stupid lady.
jokes.setup: Sexual proposition disguised as card game
jokes.punchline: Calls Marge stupid for rejecting him
jokes.explanation: Standard sleazy character behavior, serves story but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Where's the package? Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to deliver package to get inside
jokes.punchline: Guard realizes there's no actual package
jokes.explanation: Simple deception discovery, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Love of not being arrested.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he acted out of love
jokes.punchline: Specifically love of avoiding consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty about his selfish motivations, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: Of course. (Liquid gurgling) (gulping) no wonder they call it 'the good book.'
jokes.setup: Asking permission to read from Bible
jokes.punchline: Actually drinking alcohol hidden in Bible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about addiction and religious hypocrisy

jokes.text: 'Cause we're packin'! Arr, a couple of these and your first mate turns into reese witherspoon.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain at rehab facility
jokes.punchline: Alcohol makes his first mate look like actress
jokes.explanation: Sea Captain's nautical perspective on alcohol's effects with random celebrity reference

jokes.text: Arr, a couple of these and your first mate turns into reese witherspoon.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain describing alcohol effects
jokes.punchline: Specific celebrity transformation reference
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist character moment with specific pop culture reference

jokes.text: What's with all those rappin' grannies in the movies? If I ever start rappin', just shoot me in the head.
jokes.setup: Moe complaining about movie trends
jokes.punchline: Extreme reaction to hypothetical rapping scenario
jokes.explanation: Moe's curmudgeonly take on pop culture with characteristic overreaction

jokes.text: What I enjoyed was spending time with my husband. You're hooked on love, marge. I know that feelin'. Nine months later, seymour plopped outta me.
jokes.setup: Marge realizing she enjoys time with Homer
jokes.punchline: Agnes connects love to her pregnancy with Seymour
jokes.explanation: Agnes's typical oversharing about her pregnancy in inappropriate context

jokes.text: I woulda kept walking, but there were cops everywhere.
jokes.setup: Agnes describing giving birth to Seymour
jokes.punchline: Would have abandoned him if not for police presence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about maternal abandonment delivered with Agnes's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: My name is marge S. And I'm a homer-holic! You're drinkin' homer-hol? I'll take a swig.
jokes.setup: Marge declaring addiction to Homer like AA meeting
jokes.punchline: Someone thinks it's an actual drinkable substance
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with addict misunderstanding metaphorical addiction as literal substance

jokes.text: I'm afraid what I like can't be swallowed, sniffed or smoked. Hmm. Then you better inject it between my toes, 'cause my mom checks my arms!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her addiction isn't chemical
jokes.punchline: Patient suggests toe injection to avoid detection
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing severe addiction behavior and family enabling

jokes.text: He's a chronic alcoholic and you're in complete denial. Aw, shut up, captain bringdown!
jokes.setup: Counselor giving accurate assessment
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses professional help as negativity
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical resistance to professional help and reality

jokes.text: For you I'll give up rum-based cocktails. Except mojitos and rum and coke. Well, that's no promise at all! It's important for me to see you take at least one step.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to give up rum cocktails
jokes.punchline: Immediately lists major exceptions that negate promise
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer negotiation - makes meaningless promise with huge loopholes

jokes.text: For you, I will give up all clear liquors. Really? Even zima? Hey, I only drink that when I'm already drunk.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to give up clear liquors
jokes.punchline: Admits he only drinks Zima when already intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's drinking habits - Zima as desperation drink

jokes.text: Hey, anything's possible with a little help from my bible. Oh, no! It's a real one! No! Why, god, why?
jokes.setup: Homer referencing his Bible for strength
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's actual Bible, not alcohol flask
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with escalating despair - Homer's genuine religious crisis over sobriety
